observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Accountant makes certain that proper information was given to her staff handing the Special Education Fund and Trust Fund books as regards the recording of the Due from and Due to Other Funds accounts to ensure that the reciprocal accounts will render the same year-end amounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant stated that Intra-Agency accounts will be adjusted accordingly.
observations.reason: The concerned staff on her Office responsible for Special Education Fund and Trust Fund books failed to set up corresponding Due to/from Other Funds account when she herself recorded either of such on the General Fund books, hence resulting to the noted variance on the recorded amounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned municipal officials to effect the actual cash transfers of any unexpended balance of the LDRRMF for QRF and DRRMF-MOOE every end of the year to the Trust Fund books pursuant to the above regulation.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO Officer, with the concurrence of the Municipal Mayor, noted the above recommendations.
observations.reason: Municipal Accountant set up the balance of QRF for transfer as of year-end through the entry, debit to Transfer of Unspent Current Year DRRM to the Trust Funds and credit to Due to Other Funds while the actual cash transfer was not made before the closing of books of accounts. No entry was made for MF-MOOE transfer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepares and submits the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF to the Audit Team to facilitate the review and monitoring of the sources and utilization of the fund.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO Officer, with the concurrence of the Municipal Mayor, had notified the Municipal Accountant as regards the above recommendation.
observations.reason: Reports were not regularly prepared and submitted during the year due to bulk of other work assignments; missed submissions to Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the Audit Team the missing DV, otherwise, transactions shall be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction:
Non-submission of the said DV is a violation of the mentioned auditing laws and rules and regulations and prevented the Audit Team from ascertaining the validity and propriety of the disbursements.
Inquiry with the concerned staff at the Accounting Office disclosed that due to other work assignment, she was not able to submit all the DVs on time. Nevertheless, she promised to forward the noted DV immediately.
observations.reason: DV not submitted as of audit; staff committed to forward the missing DV.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. validate the propriety of the grant of bonuses and incentives; b. ensure that awards are given only when evaluation and accomplishments justify the grant; and c. comply with Civil Service Commission (CSC) Memorandum Circular No. 01 s. 2001 and other relevant guidelines.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant forwarded letter dated June 7, 2022 reiterating that an SB resolution was submitted authorizing the grant of one-time rice subsidy to augment high cost of living of workers. She asked for reconsideration stating the grant was in good faith. Copies of ordinances from other LGUs were provided as reference. Documentation submitted to the Audit Team for review.
observations.reason: Local ordinance submitted but lacks clear legal basis under national law; matter awaits final audit resolution.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. require the Inventory Committee, Office of the Municipal Accountant and the
OIC-GSO to work coordinately on the conduct and completion of the one-time
cleansing of PPE accounts;
b. instruct the Office of the Municipal Accountant and OIC-GSO to work on all
causes of discrepancies towards the reconciliation of their respective records;
c. order the Office of the Municipal Accountant to prepare Property Ledger Cards
for all PPEs; and
d. require the Office of the OIC-GSO to prepare Property Cards and Property
Acknowledgement Receipt for all PPEs.

We strongly reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and Municipal GSO
with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for
each road projects;
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed property ledger
cards for Local Road Networks and disclose the information for the said
property in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the above cited
regulations;
c. General Services Officer to prepare and maintain road networks property cards
and to submit Report on Local Road Network; and
d. Inventory Committee to conduct inventory count of all the Municipality’s road
network systems properly segregated per component and accordingly submit a
Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System as of the end of the
year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

• Municipal Accountant to:
a. verify and tally its records pertaining to the noted P30,956.07 variance
between its General Ledger and Schedule of Depreciation;
b. verify the 25 units of transportation equipment being computed with
depreciation which are not found on the report of the GSO through
supporting the same with procurement documents and acknowledgement
receipt for equipment to establish their identity for reconciliation with the
records of the GSO;
c. review items listed on the Schedule of Depreciation and properly classify
improvements recorded to the specific Motor Vehicles;
d. provide acquisition cost for the purchase of 5 units motorcycle for Traffic
Management Division and Office of the Economic Enterprise Development
and Management to compute depreciation;
e. reclassify the Mini Dump Truck with plate number RKN 492 amounting to
P750,000.00 to Construction and Heavy Equipment account;
f. confirm if the donated Dental Mobile Vehicle (FAW Jiefang Qingdao)
amounting to P4,850,000.00 was really not taken up in the books of accounts
and accordingly locate documents to record the same; and
g. reclassify the four units recorded under Other Transportation Equipment to
appropriate Construction and Heavy Equipment account.

• General Services Officer to:
a. locate the supporting documents of the 6 units of vehicles out of the 17
unrecorded items in the books of the Municipal Government and accordingly
42
verify their correct acquisition costs to tally records with the Accounting;
and
b. separately report those items taken up under the Other Transportation
Equipment and Construction and Heavy Equipment accounts from the List
of Motor Vehicles.

• Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to:
a. reconcile respective records as to the noted difference of P18,787.58 bearing
in mind the proper classification and right details of the items listed on both
records;
b. provide complete and definite details regarding each item such as brand,
plate number or other distinguished feature of the vehicles being logged on
each record to facilitate ease of reconciliation;
c. locate documents pertaining to the donated two motor vehicles and take up
its fair value on the books of accounts for computation of depreciation; and
d. confirm to which account the streetsweeper truck and farm tractor were
recorded and separate such to the List of Motor Vehicles.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare the list of unreleased checks every year-end in
time for the closing of the books and submit to the Municipal Accountant; and
b. Municipal Accountant to restore to the Cash in Bank and recognize proper
liability accounts corresponding to the unreleased checks at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal General Services
Officer to secure immediately the documents pertinent to the six Municipal vehicles
and process the transfer of name to the Municipal Government.

Also, require the Municipal GSO to exert all efforts for the yearly renewal of
registration of all motor vehicles.

Lastly, prioritized the process of obtaining the corresponding red plate number for
the motor vehicles of the Municipality to ensure proper handling and maintenance
of the property as required by existing regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
provide the Audit Team with the schedule of disbursements pertaining to the
donated Motor Vehicles to the Barangay whether in cash or units of vehicles.

Also, instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal GSO to locate documents
pertaining to the donation made and the Certificate of Registration and Official
Receipt of the subject vehicles and furnish the Barangay Government for them to
recognize the received asset on their records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the balances of RPT and SET Receivables and their
reciprocal accounts with total delinquency tax and effect the necessary
adjustments thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
evaluate and compare the amount of collectibles and payables with the records of
the Provincial Government to ascertain the legality and validity of both claims so
both parties may make the necessary adjustment on their proper books of accounts
to facilitate financial statements free of any material misrepresentation that could
affect the fairness of presentation of the accounts in the financial statements of the
Municipality.

We reiterated that the Municipal Accountant makes certain that proper information
was given to her staff handing the Special Education Fund and Trust Fund books
as regards the recording of the Due from and Due to Other Funds accounts to
ensure that the reciprocal accounts will render the same year-end amounts.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Assessor and
Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents
required under Section 13 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to properly substantiate
the above disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to:

a. submit to the Audit Team the lists of projects under the Continuing
Appropriations;
b. judiciously plan on how to maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF especially
those under the Mitigation Fund-Capital Outlay; and
c. reevaluate programs included under the MF-Capital Outlay in order to achieve
the goal/target of the Municipality regarding disaster relief, recovery, and risk
reduction and management.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned municipal
officials to effect the actual cash transfers of any unexpended balance of the
LDRRMF for QRF and DRRMF-MOOE every end of the year to the Trust Fund books
pursuant to the above regulation.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor propose to the Local Sanggunian the
enactment for availability to other social services of the prior years’ unexpended
balance of LDRRMF which remained unutilized after five years and the reversion of
the said fund from Trust Fund to unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make
the necessary journal entry to present the correct year-end balances of the affected
accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality. Henceforth, record under
the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account the DRRM funds received from National
Government.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepares and submits the Monthly
Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF to the Audit Team to facilitate
the review and monitoring of the sources and utilization of the fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
observe the proper journal entries for recording of receipts and disbursements
under the Trust Fund. Refrain from making shortcut entries for proper monitoring
of the balances of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to
submit regularly the monthly CRAAF to the Audit Team to ensure that all
accountable forms, e.g. official receipts, checks, Community Tax Certificates and
cash tickets received and issued are properly accounted for and to prevent the
fraudulent use of the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
immediately submit to the Audit Team the missing DV, otherwise, transactions shall
be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. create an Inventory Committee for the conduct of complete and detailed physical count of all the properties taking into account the provisions mandated under COA Circular No. 2020-006; b. instruct the Inventory Committee to properly utilize the required inventory reports stated on the above-mentioned regulations; c. order the Office of the General Services Officer (GSO)/Municipal Treasurer to prepare property cards and Property Acknowledgment Receipt for all PPEs; d. require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO/Municipal Treasurer to coordinate and reconcile all causes of discrepancies towards the reconciliation of their respective records to ascertain the accuracy of the PPE balances in the financial statements; e. in spite the recommendation herein, strictly enforce Section 122(2) of Presidential Decree (PD) 1445 relative to the withholding of salary of concerned officials for failure to submit reconciled and accurate inventory reports; and f. instruct the Municipal Budget Officer for the enactment of the Sangguniang Bayan to appropriate funds in ensuing year for the filling up of the General Services Officer to have a permanent official who will assume the duties and responsibilities in the office and to address the perennial issues relative to the supply and property management of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Administrator directed the Inventory Committee to conduct one time cleansing of PPE accounts and to continuously reconcile PPE records of the Accounting Division with that of the Property Management Section. The Accountant and the Property Officer will work to resolve the huge amount of variance between the PPE Ledger Cards and the RPCPPE. Also, the said Offices were directed to prepare necessary forms such as PPE Ledger Cards, Property Cards and Property Acknowledgment Receipts for all PPEs.
The Municipal Accountant forwarded to the Audit Team on April 29, 2022, via email, a schedule of reconciliation between their records and inventory report. However, the said report, only accounted Flood Control System, Water Supply System, Park, Plazas and Monuments, School Building, Communication Equipment, Construction and Heavy Equipment, Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment, Military, Police and Security Equipment, Other Transportation Equipment, and Buildings account.
observations.reason: To comply with COA’s recommendatio n in CY 2022. One-time cleansing of PPE accounts to be conducted in CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and Municipal GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for each road projects; b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed property ledger cards for Local Road Networks and disclose the information for the said property in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the above cited regulations; c. General Services Officer to prepare and maintain road networks property cards and to submit Report on Local Road Network; and d. Inventory Committee to conduct inventory count of all the Municipality’s road network systems properly segregated per component and accordingly submit a Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System as of the end of the year.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality of Padre Garcia aims to comply with circulars and guidelines on Local Road Networks. Despite of the challenges on data gathering, the Report of Local Road Network was accomplished and shall be continuously updated with complete description and cost segregation of road components for every road project.
The Municipal Mayor directed he concerned offices to work closely and produce the prescribed ledger cards, property cards and reports on local road networks.
The Municipal Accountant will prepare & maintain the prescribed property ledger cards for Local Road Networks in accordance with COA cited regulations upon receipt of the report from the Municipal Engineering Office
observations.reason: A report was forwarded to the GSO but not to the Municipal Accounting and no property ledger cards and disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements were prepared. The report was still not complete as to the description and cost segregation of each road components.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • Inventory Committee to: a. conduct complete and detailed physical count of all the Municipality’s inventory items at year- first end; and b. properly utilize the required inventory reports such as AIR and SSMI. • Municipal GSO/Municipal Treasurer to: a. prepare stock cards for all inventory items maintained by the Municipality; b. record in the stock card inventories received based on the AIR and submit to the local accountant copies of the AIR, Purchase Order and delivery receipts for the drawing of Journal voucher (JV) to record the Inventories in the Journal of Procurements Received and in the inventory ledger cards; and c. prepare Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) upon issuance of inventory items and summarize them in RSMI. • Municipal Accountant to: a. upon receipt of the AIR and other supporting documents draw a JV to record the Inventories in the Journal of Procurements Received and in the inventory ledger cards; and b. use weighted average as the costing method. • Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds in ensuing year for the filling up of the General Services Officer to have a permanent official who will assume the duties and responsibilities in the office and to address the perennial issues relative to the supply and property management of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Administrator, with the concurrence of the Municipal Mayor, directed the Inventory Committee to conduct one time cleansing of PPE accounts and to continuously reconcile PPE records of the Accounting Division with that of the Property Management Section. The concerned offices will work to resolve the variances and comply with the recommendations.
The Municipal GSO prepared the required RIS but failed to summarize the required reports in RSMI.
The Municipal GSO prepared some inventory stock cards for some inventory items only.
The Municipal Accountant complied with this regulation. The Municipal Accountant recorded inventories in compliance with the regulations. The Municipal GSO prepared the required RIS but failed to summarize them in RSMI. The Municipal Budget Officer is in the process of designating a permanent officer in the Office of the Municipal GSO.
observations.reason: The GSO prepared RIS and some stock cards but failed to prepare the required RSMI and stock cards for all inventory items. The Municipal GSO has no staff to help her prepare the required reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the breakdown of the total delinquent taxes as to the principal amount, interest and penalty to come with a comparable data with that of the Municipal Accountant’s record; b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the balances of RPT and SET Receivables and their reciprocal accounts with total delinquency tax and effect the necessary adjustments thereof; c. Municipal Accountant to adopt the new deferred recording the total amount collectible at the beginning of the year in accordance with COA Circular No. 2021-007 dated September 6, 2021; and d. Municipal Treasurer to monitor payment of real property tax due for the current year and exhaust all efforts and devise strategies to collect all delinquent real property taxes and encourage the tax payers to pay their dues on time.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, both confirmed that they will work on the reconciliation of accounts.
observations.reason: A difference of P230,584.00 for each fund was still noted in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to evaluate and compare the amount of collectibles and payables with the records of the Provincial Government to ascertain the legality and validity of both claims so both parties may make the necessary adjustment on their proper books of accounts to facilitate financial statements free of any material misrepresentation that could affect the fairness of presentation of the accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant reasoned out that Due from LGUs amounting to P1,289.22 has been booked up on their records even before she came as the Municipal Accountant and she haven’t found any other record/document pertaining to such–the same thing with Due to LGUs in the amount of P151.20 (GF) and P(0.05) (Trust Fund). Meanwhile, Due to LGUs (Province) having variance are reconciled/remitted regularly during the current year except for the year end balances which are usually settled/liquidated at the first quarter of the ensuing year.
Additionally, Due from LGUs totaling P2,014.23 (SEF Local - P 0.01, Citation Fee - Local - P725.00 and Overpayment of CTC Province - P 1,289.22) were requested for write off per communication dated June 8, 2022 of the Municipal Mayor.
observations.reason: Discrepancies still noted in CY 2021 due to unavailability of proper supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents required under Section 13 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to properly substantiate the above disbursements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor propose to the Local Sanggunian the enactment for availability to other social services of the prior years’ unexpended balance of LDRRMF which remained unutilized after five years and the reversion of the said fund from Trust Fund to unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO Officer, with the concurrence of the Municipal Mayor, had notified the Local Sanggunian with regard the above recommendation.
observations.reason: Notification to Local Sanggunian made; reversion pending enactment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary journal entry to present the correct year-end balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality. Henceforth, record under the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account the DRRM funds received from National Government.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO Officer, with the concurrence of the Municipal Mayor, had notified the Municipal Accountant as regards the above recommendation.
observations.reason: Out of total received P977,666.00, P974,135.00 was utilized and a balance of P3,531.00 remains for return to the National Treasury; misclassification noted and correcting entries to be made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper journal entries for recording of receipts and disbursements under the Trust Fund. Refrain from making shortcut entries for proper monitoring of the balances of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will use the prescribed format and journal entries for recording receipts and disbursements under the Trust Fund for CY 2022.
observations.reason: Accounting treatment was adjusted moving forward; omissions and shortcut entries noted in CY2021 and will be corrected in CY2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to submit regularly the monthly CRAAF to the Audit Team to ensure that all accountable forms, e.g. official receipts, checks, Community Tax Certificates and cash tickets received and issued are properly accounted for and to prevent the fraudulent use of the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer committed to follow the submission of the required report starting from March 2022 onwards. In fact, report for March, April and May 2022 were already forwarded to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: CRAAF not submitted during CY2021; commitment made to submit monthly reports starting March 2022 and some reports forwarded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairperson of the Executive Committee together with the Gender Focal Point System and the Technical Group to: a. exert active efforts in accomplishing the targeted and focused PPAs; b. ensure the proper mainstreaming of GAD related budget activities in all departments/offices of the Municipality; and c. coordinate with the Municipal Human Resources Management Officer to require all personnel to undergo training on gender sensitivity, human rights and gender responsiveness especially those holding positions involving protection and defense of women against gender-based violence, counselling and trauma healing.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Management of the Government of Padre Garcia complied with the regulations. Targeted and planned PPAs accomplishing P8,445,000.00 were indicated but only P2,438,415.00 or 28.87% was utilized as of December 31, 2021. The Municipal Accountant and other offices are coordinating to mainstream GAD related activities and trainings. One Gender Sensitivity Training was conducted.
observations.reason: Only 3 of 25 GAD programs fully implemented; partial utilization of GAD budget; some trainings and mainstreaming pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to determine the nature of acquisition of the said land owned by the Municipality, and subsequently record its equivalent peso value to present fairly the Land account in the Municipality's financial statements; b. further, refund the excess payment amounting to P346,580.00. c. pursue the titling of the 11 parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to establish absolute ownership over the said property; and d. submit documents to support the purchase and effect necessary correction/ refund of excess payment where applicable.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Accountant and other concerned offices are working to determine the nature of acquisition and are coordinating with CENRO, DAR and BIR for titling and issuance of e-CAR and other required documents. Some titles are at final step of processing; refunds and reconciliations being pursued. Submitted documents and communications to resolve outstanding items.
observations.reason: Some parcels are titled or in process of titling; some excess payments identified and processes for refund and documentation ongoing; unavailability of supporting documents for some items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor re-program and utilize the balance of unexpended balances to other developmental projects and ensure implementation to achieve the targeted outputs of the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: Unexpended balances of P27,383,893.06 were disbursed for CY 2021 per SCBAA submitted by the agency. The Municipality is reprogramming and disbursing funds as needed for development projects.
observations.reason: There still remain unutilized balances of P26,581,015.30 per SCBAA; reprogramming in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to: a. create an adequate organizational structure and provide adequate permanent machineries and equipment to the Solid Waste Management Office; b. recruit/appoint qualified technical personnel; c. ensure presence of proper office and storages; and d. ensure implementation and monitoring of the solid waste programs including maintenance of equipment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Government is in the process of strengthening the Solid Waste Management Office. A total of 14 permanent and 36 casuals/job orders were assigned. They are housed in temporary office pending the completion of new municipal building. Also, 2 units of tricycle, 2 dump trucks, 2 garbage compactors and 1 streetsweeper truck were being utilized by the said Office.
observations.reason: Staffing and equipment improved but permanent office and full complement of required machinery pending; some equipment in use.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure strict compliance with the General Appropriations Act (GAA) provision that monthly take-home pay of employee shall not be lower than Five Thousand Pesos (P5,000.00), and discontinue deductions on monthly loan amortizations of government from the personnel’s payroll other than those mandated by law.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO, Municipal Treasurer and AAO reviewed payroll inputs and ensured that net take home pay was not reduced below P5,000.00. Remittances to GSIS and other authorized deductions are made regularly. Adjustments and monitoring put in place.
observations.reason: Payroll practices adjusted to ensure compliance; remittances made properly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor include among its top priorities the construction of a conference building for the safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents generated by the various departments/offices of the Municipality, and to provide an office space and storeroom for the audit team.
observations.managementAction: An office space was provided to the Audit Team. The Municipal Mayor and the honorable members of the Sanggunian promised to provide the required building. The Municipality provided temporary accommodation and committed to provide permanent structure upon completion of the new municipal building.
observations.reason: Office space and temporary storage provided; construction of permanent facility planned and committed.

• Municipal Accountant to:
a. upon receipt of the AIR and other supporting documents such as Purchase
Orders, Delivery Receipts and/or Supplier’s Invoice, draw a Journal voucher
(JV) to record the Inventories in the Journal of Procurements Received and in
the inventory ledger cards; and
b. use weighted average method during the year in costing inventories carried on
stock and measure inventories for consumption and distribution at the end of
the year at the lower of cost and current replacement cost as stated on the
above Manual.
• Municipal Budget Officer to:
a. appropriate funds in ensuing year for enactment of the Sangguniang Bayan for
the filling up of the General Services Officer to have a permanent official who
will assume the duties and responsibilities in the office and to address the
perennial issues relative to the supply and property management of the
Municipality.

We strongly recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare the breakdown of the total delinquent taxes as to
the principal amount, interest and penalty to come with a comparable data with
that of the Municipal Accountant’s record;
c. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the balances of RPT
and SET Receivables and the total delinquency taxes and effect the necessary
adjustments, if any;
d. Municipal Accountant to adopt the new guidelines in recording the total amount
collectible at the beginning of the year; and
e. Municipal Treasurer to monitor payment of real property tax due for the current
year and exhaust all efforts and devise strategies to collect all delinquent real
property taxes and encourage the tax payers to pay their dues on time.

We strongly reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
exert time and effort to fully coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and have a
direct sit down review and analysis of the accounts to determine the causes of the
long over due deficiencies. If claims are fully documented and establishes regularity
of transactions, then settlements/collections should be made by the Municipality. In
case exhaustion of all possible remedies and diligent efforts, the issue remain
unsettled, resort to prescribed procedures on write-off of receivables and/or reversion
of accounts payables upon presentation of the required documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to deduct the correct amount of premiums and loan
amortizations due to avoid incurrence of penalties;
b. Municipal Treasurer remit the same immediately to GSIS;
c. Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) to ensure compliance with the required
employees’ net pay after deducting the loan amortizations prior to approving
loans; and
d. Municipal Accountant and AAO to work hand in hand and coordinate with the GSIS
for the reconciliation of records to determine the correctness of the unpaid
premiums of PS and GS with a total amount of ₱435,282.92.

We acclaimed the Management of the Municipal Government of Padre Garcia for the
competent and rapid implementation of the identified programs, activities and projects
towards the achievement of BGCM’s objective.

However, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant and the BAC, thru its Secretariat to immediately present for
audit the lacking documents to substantiate aforementioned BGCM transactions and
ensure that all documentary requirements for emergency purchase are complied with
and properly filled out before certifying as to completeness of the supporting
documents of the disbursement vouchers; and
b. Municipal Accountant to use the appropriate accounts in recording transactions in
compliance with Volume III of the GAM for LGUs prescribing the Revised Chart of
Accounts for Local Government Units.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the:
• MSWDO to:
a. in coordination with the barangay officials, to continuously work hand-in-hand
in maintaining/updating profile of families under its jurisdiction to properly and
readily identify the eligible beneficiaries of various programs/projects which
may be initiated either by government or private sector; and
b. provide justification on the result of the validation of three (3) beneficiaries
being able to receive two SAP tranches despite their ineligibilities.
• Municipal Accountant and MSWDO to:
a. submit the necessary documents attached to the payroll to support the receipt
of the subsidy.

We would like to laud the efforts undertaken by the MDRRMO and the whole
Municipality in responding to the different calamities and disasters that struck not
only the Municipality but also its nearby areas. Likewise, they were able to prepare
their long term LDRRMF Plan which was fully integrated with the Local Development
Plan and Comprehensive Land-Use Plan of the Municipality. Further, the Municipality
was fully compliant on the immediate transfer to the Trust Liability-DRRMF account
under the Trust Fund the unexpended QRF and MF-MOOE for CY 2020. However, for
the above noted deficiencies, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• LDRRM Officer to:
a. ensure that the list of projects and activities which are to be charged to the
unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF under the Trust Fund books is included
in the Annual LDRRMFIP to be prepared yearly;
b. present in the AIP/LDRRMFIP for the coming years the allocation for disaster
mitigation, prevention and preparedness with details as to all of the projects
and activities to be funded; and
c. make certain the maximum utilization of current Mitigation Funds and
coordinate with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator for the
implementation of planned programs/projects of the LDRRM Office under the
continuing appropriations.
• Local Development Council to:
a. adhere to the provisions stated on Section 2.1 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 02 dated March 30, 2020 which mentioned that in case the COVID-
19-related PPAs and expenses are not among those included in the approved
AIP, the Local Development Council shall prepare a Supplemental Investment
Program for the purpose, to be approved by the local sanggunian.
• Municipal Accountant to:
a. prepare and certified correct the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of
LDRRMF to be submitted by the LDRRM Officer to the Audit Team on or before
the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local
Development Council;
b. account and submit DVs for the expended QRF for CY 2020 amounting to
₱2,910,363.50;
c. use the appropriate inventory accounts in recording transactions in compliance
with COA Circular No. 2015-009 prescribing the Revised Chart of Accounts for
Local Government Units;
d. comply with the recording of revenues and expenditures in the Trust Fund
books as provided under the new Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for
LGUs; and
e. submit DVs totaling ₱6,450,280.00 as provided on Annex I of this report.
• Municipal Accountant and BAC, thru its Secretariat to:
a. immediately present for audit the lacking documents to substantiate
aforementioned transactions and/or provide written explanation/justification for
the incomplete documentation.

In order to reconcile the RPCPPE with the records of the Accounting Office, we
recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Accountant to:
a. reclassify the amount debited to Construction in Progress account to
Advances to Contractors totaling ₱2,844,450.00;
• GSO-Designate/Acting Municipal Treasurer to:
a. use and completely fill out the RPCPPE format prescribed under the NGAS
Manual (Annex 49);
b. complete physical count of all properties of the Municipality on all funds;
c. use of new chart of accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 on
the classification of counted properties;
d. maintain complete property cards and ARE for all PPEs, and;
• Municipal Accountant and GSO Designate/Acting Municipal Treasurer to:
a. evaluate and work coordinately on all causes of discrepancies towards the
reconciliation to ascertain the accuracy of the PPE balances in the financial
statements.
We also recommended to the Municipal Mayor that the Budget Officer appropriate funds
again in the ensuing year for the filling up of the General Services Officer to have a
permanent official who will assume the duties and responsibilities in the office and to
address the perennial issues relative to the supply and property management of the
Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed property ledger
cards for Local Road Networks and disclose the information for the said property
in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the above cited
regulations;
b. General Services Office to prepare and maintain road networks property cards
and to submit Report on Local Road Network;
c. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer
with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road
projects;
d. Inventory Committee to conduct inventory count of all the Municipality’s road
network systems and accordingly submit a Report on the Physical Count of the
Road Network System as of the end of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• Municipal GSO-Designate and Acting Municipal Treasurer to:
a. number each type of supplies and maintain Stock Cards per stock number;
b. forward copy of the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR) to the Municipal
Accountant for the preparation of the DV and recording of deliveries in the
appropriate ledger cards;
c. consolidate weekly the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) for which supplies and
materials were issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued
(SSMI) and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant together with the
original copies of RIS; and
d. use the prescribed format in reporting the physical count of inventories and
submit the same to the Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and January
31 of each year.
• Municipal Accountant to:
a. maintain Supplies Ledger Cards per stock number and record the purchases in
the appropriate ledger cards upon receipt of the AIR and other supporting
documents;
b. use the appropriate inventory account in recording the purchases and
corresponding expense account upon receipt of SSMI submitted by the GSO;
and
c. use the moving average method in costing the inventories.
Similar with that of the PPE, we repeated our recommendation with the Municipal
Mayor that the Budget Officer appropriate funds again in the ensuing year for the filling
up of the General Services Officer to have a permanent official who will assume the
duties and responsibilities in the office, and to address in totality the recurrent issues
relative to the supply and property management of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• Municipal Treasurer to:
a. prepare and submit to the Municipal Accounting Office the List of Unreleased
Checks in time for the closing of the books every year for the reversion back to
cash in bank and payable accounts, in order to fairly present the said accounts
in the financial statements; and
b. send written notice to the payees for the existence of stale checks and require
submission of requirements to facilitate replacement of new checks; and
• Municipal Accountant to:
a. effect the necessary adjustments of Unreleased Checks treated as reconciling
items/ outstanding checks at the end of the year/closing of books, as well as
the reversion of stale checks for an accurate and reliable accounts of Cash in
Bank and Accounts Payable balances in the financial statements.

We strongly reiterated that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to initiate the request for write-off of the long outstanding
balances of Due from NGAs, Due from LGUs, Other Receivables-General Fund and
Special Education Fund using the documents herein noted following the guidelines
and procedures prescribed under the aforementioned COA Circular; and
b. the Municipal MSWDO to prepare a schedule classifying the debtor under SEAP
into active and inactive status for which active accounts have the probability of
being collected while the inactive accounts will be documented for proper write-
off.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Accountant and her
staff to continuously reconcile their records on receivables/payables in the books of the
Municipality for intra-agency accounts and coordinate with the Provincial
Accountant for account Due from/Due to LGUs to establish the correct balances in the
corresponding subsidiary ledgers and to ascertain the reliability of both records in order
that both the Municipality and Province may make the necessary adjustments on their
respective books of accounts. Moreso, settle immediately the established payable of
each fund for a fair presentation of the cash position of the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Finance Committee to:
a. carefully plan and prepare analytically the proposed annual budget on the basis of
realistic projection of revenues and undertake proper review of past year’s
efficiency performances; and
b. monitor monthly collection so as to keep tract of the status and evaluate whether
targets are attained. Analyze the reasons for the shortfall in collections and adopt
measures or alternate solutions to avoid incurrence of material shortfall in
projections in the ensuing years, so that other plans and programs of the
municipality would be carried out and implemented.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Accountant to make
the required adjustment in the books and make certain that in the ensuing year,
subsequent shares from the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) - Lotto
Draws and Small Town Lottery (STL) of the Municipality is properly accounted as
income and recorded using “Share from PCSO (4-04-01-020)” account pursuant to
COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the PRAISE Committee to submit
documents that will validly support their grant of the incentive. Otherwise, the amount
tolling ₱793,499.53 will not be allowed in audit.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Praise Committee:
a. evaluate the performance of the employees by means of the agency’s own
established system of incentives and awards;
b. ensure that the specific policies and guidelines in monitoring, evaluation and
mechanism for recognizing the awardees are in place before granting awards to the
employees;
c. ensure that monetary awards were given only when accomplishments and personal
efforts resulted in monetary savings and it does not exceed 20% of the savings
generated; and
d. strictly comply with the policy and regulations pursuant to CSC Memorandum
Circular No. 01 s 2001.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. direct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator to properly plan and prioritize all development projects to prevent
occurrence of issuance of Suspension Orders that hinders the timely achievement
of socio-economic development for the constituents;
b. require the concerned officials to submit justification on issuing suspension
orders not in accordance with Item 9 of Annex E;
c. plan for the immediate action/measures to address the delay in the completion,
operation and actual usage of the above enumerated projects;
d. submit copy of Statement of Work Accomplished for unperformed portion signed
by the Municipal Engineer and duly acknowledged by the contractor on the
number of days delayed; and
e. provide the Audit Team with report of action taken for the aforementioned delayed
projects to apprise of the liquidated damages collected due the government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. Municipal Engineer to submit Approved design plans and technical specifications
for all infrastructure projects being implemented by the Municipality;
b. Bids and Award Committee (BAC) Secretariat and others concerned officials to
strictly abide with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 particularly on the
submission of complete documentation and relative to the deficiencies noted in
order to ensure the validity and propriety of procurements made by the Municipal
Government; and
c. Concerned municipal officials to immediately submit the lacking documents as
required for further evaluation of the procurement of infrastructure and
equipment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the Municipal Accountant to prepare individual financial statements with
complete subsidiary ledgers for each economic enterprise being operated by the
Municipality which should form part of the schedules of the General Fund; and
b. instruct the Municipal Budget Officer in coordination with the OEEDM Head to
separately appropriate funds for each economic enterprise in the ensuing year to
facilitate the reconciliation with the records of the Accounting office.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. MDRRMO to prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF
certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and submit on or before the 15th day
after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development
Council. Likewise, the MDRRMO to implement and monitor at the intended period
of its implementation the planned programs/projects to ensure that the purpose for
which the special trust fund was established are met.
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to effect the
recognition and transfer of cash from the General Fund to the Trust Fund Book of
the unutilized QRF for CY 2019, in the amount of ₱3,006,935.93.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the Chairperson of the Executive
Committee together with the Gender Focal Point System and the Technical Working
Group to:
a. exert active efforts in accomplishing the targeted and planned Client-focused and
Organization-focused PPAs;
b. ensure the proper mainstreaming of GAD related activities in all
departments/offices of the Municipality; and
c. coordinate with the Municipal Human Resources Management Officer to require
all personnel to undergo training on gender sensitivity, human rights and gender
responsiveness especially those holding positions involving protection and
defense of women against gender-based violence, counselling and trauma
healing.

observations.managementAction: An Inventory Committee was created and the GSO-Designate was instructed to conduct a complete inventory taking. Reiterated, as amended on Part II of this report.
observations.reason: A RPCPPE for CY 2019 was submitted but GSO-Designate was not complete. The records between the Accounting Office and GSO remain unreconciled difference of ₱84,768,752.48 as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• direct the Municipal Assessor to pursue the titling of the 11 parcels of land in the name of the Municipality in order to establish absolute ownership over the said municipal property; and
• require the Appropriation for the titling of the land. Appropriation for the titling of land totalling 5.8M (Office of the Mayor – 2.8M and Office of Assessor – 3M) was made for CY 2020.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, require the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the nature of the circumstance as to the mode of acquisition of land not covered by TCT so as not to mislead users of financial statements and appropriate valuation be taken up in the books.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant are continuously determine the nature of acquisition of the 11 parcels of land owned by the Municipality. The land where the municipal compound, gym and park are located are already titled while the land where the municipal cemetery, temporary settlement area (at the back of the church), artesian well and livestock auction market have pending application for Special Patent per letter dated July 10, 2019 and January 2, 2020, respectively. The remaining land for market site is awaiting for the approved plan from the geodetic engineer contracted by the Municipality to be able to process its title while the location of Padre V. Garcia National High School is for coordination with the said school for possible transfer of ownership. Appropriation for the titling of land totalling 5.8M (Office of the Mayor – 2.8M and Office of Assessor – 3M) was made for CY 2020.
observations.reason: There still remain an unreconciled parcel of land.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officials to:
• adopt measures and strategies to improve collection efficiency, improve informed collection, regular operation and collection of Real Property Taxes, not only current taxes but also the delinquent taxes in order to generate more income; and
• the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to check from the Provincial Treasurer the status of the rest of the reported tax delinquencies and the action taken by the latter in the imposition of the required remedies in the collection of real property taxes against said delinquent taxpayers.
observations.managementAction: Collection campaign was conducted. They conducted a house to house tax campaign and posted List of Delinquent Tax Payers in all conspicuous places. Coordination was regularly conducted with the Provincial Treasurer. Verified that massive tax campaign, although, there are taxpayers who remain uncollected on their address or in the location of the delinquent properties.
observations.reason: Despite efforts, there still remain numerous delinquent property owners who could not be located; some delinquent taxes remain uncollected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Accountant and her staff to continuously reconcile their records on receivables/payables with the Provincial Accountant and reconcile the records with that of the Province to identify the causes of discrepancies in the reciprocal accounts and to ascertain the legality and validity of both claims so both parties may make the necessary adjustments on their respective books of accounts, if warranted. Likewise, effect the immediate settlement of obligations of each fund for a fair presentation of the cash position for the three funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant specified that Intra agency accounts of Due from and Due to Other Funds will be settled promptly to fairly present the Municipality’s financial statements. She added that Due from and Due to LGUs under the Municipality and Province books were looked into for possible reconciliation but for lack of supporting documents to prove the existence of these long overdue accounts, they are faced with the question of who will make the necessary adjustment, if any.
observations.reason: There still remain unreconciled differences between the two records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:

a. ensure the 20% Development or appropriated out of Internal Revenue Allotment for CY 2019 of ₱8,700,000.00 (36% (4 out of 8 projects) of the total budget for CY 2019 was disbursed on the said appropriation year; remain unimplemented of the total appropriation); 
b. come up with a well-defined mechanism in order that appropriate results and outputs from the identified priority programs and projects be delivered efficiently and effectively to the public on time; and 
c. re-program and utilize the ₱6,515,421.05 unexpended balances to other developmental projects.
observations.managementAction: ₱8,700,000.00 or 36% (4 out of 8 projects) of ₱15,148,903.60 total budget for CY 2019 was obligated on the said appropriation year. A total of ₱6,515,421.05 was utilized out of continuing appropriation for 20% per SCBAA submitted by the agency. A total of ₱7,281,510.19 of unutilized balances of the appropriation as of December 31, 2019 per SCBAA submitted by the agency.
observations.reason: There still remain unutilized balances of ₱7,281,510.19 of the 20% Development Fund as of December 31, 2019 per SCBAA submitted by the agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to verify and evaluate the accounts involved in the above transactions and to prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the adjustments for the stale checks to present the correct and the reliable balance of the Cash in Bank and Accounts Payable accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared JEV No. 400 dated April 23, 2019 to effect the adjustments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Technical Working Group (TWG) to:
• evaluate the adequacy of the manpower and equipment on all projects to be awarded to the winning bidders to order the winning contractor to increase its manpower and/or equipment to be employed in the project awarded to it or to disqualify them from obtaining several award or contract; 
• strictly observe/monitor the bidders with on-going projects with other government agencies disclosed in the Statement of On-Going and Completed projects to comply with the bidding requirements; and
• conduct the required processes and procedures in all stages of the bidding in the procurement of infrastructure and comply strictly with all documentary requirements as mandated by RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: According to the Municipal Engineer, there are enough manpower and equipment being utilized by the contractor aside from those enumerated in the list of implementing equipment submitted. The subject works were completed on time. The Municipal Engineer commented that they follow the rules and regulations in the procurement of goods, infrastructures and consulting services as mandated by RA No. 9184. Non-compliance of the required documents were submitted. The Municipal Management prepare Work Accomplishment Report as of June 30, 2019. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat submitted documents. The Municipal Engineer stated that work accomplishment report is being prepared by her Officer regularly.
observations.reason: There were lacking documents noted on CY 2019 contracts as of June 30, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate regular bonded employees who are holders of permanent position to act as collectors/custodians of government funds. As such, they should be properly bonded pursuant to Section 101 of PD 1445 and Section 305 (f) of RA 7160 and Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The designated regular bonded employees were assigned to act as collectors. Collections of the said employees were remitted daily to the concern regular employees.
observations.reason: Due to the lack of regular bonded employees to act as collectors, the JO/casual employees were assigned as collectors; the municipal collectors remitted daily to the concern regular employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the General Services Officer prepare the latest inventory report with complete list of all insurable properties for indorsement to the GSIS so that the appraisal thereof and the computation of the amount of insurance premiums payable by the Municipality could be readily made. Also, the insurance coverage made with a private company be renewed with the GSIS. Henceforth, strictly comply with R.A. No. 656, to ensure that the Municipality will be compensated or indemnified for property damages or losses caused by fire, earthquake and other covered calamities.
observations.managementAction: The GSO-Designate stated that insurance policies were already secured for the year properties except for three motor vehicles (Kia 2500, Hyundai Grand Starex, Toyota Hilux ambulance), Municipal Memorial Park, Building, Gymnasium Building. Inventory report is ongoing. The GSO-Designate was instructed to start the process of preparations of inventory report for all property and equipment.
observations.reason: Inventory reports were not submitted to the Audit Team; insurance secured for some properties but not all; inventory is ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Instruct the designated GSO to carefully check the procurement process to keep track the movement of stock on hand from receipt until issuance for purposes of accounting, control and accountability; initiate the preparation of stock cards for the purchased supplies; conduct periodic inventory of supplies as prescribed by the above regulation; and procure, as far as practicable and feasible, directly from the DBM-PS all anticipated common-use supplies, in bulk or three months supply and resort to local suppliers only when not available with said office which must be duly supported with certification of non-availability.
observations.managementAction: The GSO-Designate has started the preparation of stock cards for the purchased supplies and conducting periodic inventory as prescribed. Procurement of supplies in bulk is being observed.
observations.reason: Some management practices observed but documentation and complete compliance still pending.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
• submit the disbursement voucher/obligations request and other supporting documents for payables totaling ₱3,478,045.47 and support the obligation with document to the former elected officials and employees who are no longer in the service to verify the correctness of the amount booked up under Accounts Payable and Due to Officers and Employees and to support the terminal leave claims for elected officials and employees who are no longer in the service.
observations.managementAction: Documents like disbursement voucher/obligation request and other supporting documents relating to amounts of ₱3,274,819.87 were submitted by the Human Resource Officer to support the obligation with control record as of December 31, 2019. Those terminal leave claims due to elected officials and employees no longer in the service to verify correctness of the balances were submitted.
observations.reason: Terminal leave claims due to elected officials and employees no longer in service still had outstanding balances; some documentation was submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to remit the shares of the barangays from real property taxes within the period after the end of each quarter in order that the barangays could use the fund at the earliest possible time and deliver the basic services to their constituents.
observations.managementAction: Remittance of barangay shares was made every semester due to administrative arrangements; full compliance to remit quarterly will be implemented in CY 2020.
observations.reason: Remittance was not made within the five-day requirement; barangay shares were remitted every semester; full compliance targeted in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality submit legal basis that allows granting of Rice Subsidy Allowance to all municipal officials and employees to validate the propriety of the disbursement of funds, otherwise the expenditure shall not be allowed in the audit.
observations.managementAction: Sangguniang Bayan Resolution No. 76-2017 dated December 4, 2017 was submitted which was the basis for the grant of the said allowance. However, the AAR for CY 2017 opined that the said rice subsidy resolution lacked legal basis per AAR for CY 2017. The Resolution was evaluated and forwarded.
observations.reason: Despite SB Resolution, the AAR for CY 2017 concluded lack of legal basis; evaluation ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Government refer to DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in the appropriation and utilization of 20% Development Fund and ensure attainment of desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and partake nature of investment or capital expenditures.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2019, ₱8,700,000.00 or 36% (4 out of 8 projects) of the total budget was obligated on the said appropriation year; reprogramming and utilization of unexpended balances was performed; a total of ₱6,515,421.05 was utilized out continuing appropriations for 20% Development Fund as of December 31, 2019 per SCBAA submitted by the agency.
observations.reason: Programs/projects not aligned with the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1; some balances remain unutilized.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the: • OIC Municipal Treasurer to determine the actual condition of the property and establish physical existence and condition of all PPE; • Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and General Services Officer to comply with the duties and coordination responsibilities with COA Circular No. 2015-008; • Concerned Municipal officials to ensure that road networks be properly recorded in the Registry and be transferred to the books of accounts within the target dates; and • OIC-Municipal Treasurer to submit the updated Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment for all property and equipment owned by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is going on with the inventory of roads and is consolidating all the data and its components as well as the road map. The OIC-Municipal Treasurer submitted Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment for all property and equipment is ongoing. The General Services Officer complied with preparation of inventory report and the Municipal Accountant started the transfer of these assets.
observations.reason: Physical inventory still ongoing; some acknowledgements incomplete; road networks inventory consolidations in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to adopt effective and efficient tax strategies to intensify collection of Real Property Taxes, not only current taxes but also the delinquent taxes, and to send notice of delinquency to real property owners especially those with huge balances and avail of remedies by administrative action through levy on real property or judicial action.
observations.managementAction: Collection efforts were exerted; massive tax campaign and posting of List of Delinquent Tax Payers in all conspicuous places were conducted. The Municipal Treasurer informed that the list of delinquent taxpayers was forwarded to the Provincial Treasurer for appropriate action.
observations.reason: Despite collection efforts, numerous delinquent accounts remain uncollected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the balance of the reciprocal accounts in all funds and ensure that all reciprocal transactions pertaining thereto are properly and accurately recorded in the municipality's books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared JEV Nos. 300-2018-05-01660, 300-2018-05-01661 and 300-2018-05-01758 dated May 3, 2018 to effect adjustment in the corresponding reciprocal accounts and reported.
observations.reason: Some entries were made but some discrepancies remain.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GSO to create stock cards, track movement of supplies and procure from DBM-PS as appropriate.
observations.managementAction: The GSO-Designate started the preparation of stock cards and periodic inventory; procurement of supplies in bulk from DBM-PS is being observed when available.
observations.reason: Ongoing preparation of stock cards; compliance to procurement from DBM-PS being observed when feasible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended submission of disbursement vouchers/obligations and supporting documents; present evidence for terminal leave claims; ensure correctness.
observations.managementAction: Disbursement vouchers and supporting documents totaling ₱3,274,819.87 were submitted by the Human Resource Officer to support the obligation. Some terminal leave claims were submitted.
observations.reason: Some documents submitted but terminal leave claims of former employees still need validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended remitting barangay shares within the prescribed period to allow barangays to utilize funds timely.
observations.managementAction: Remittances were made each semester due to administrative arrangements; the Municipality indicated full compliance would be observed in CY 2020.
observations.reason: Remittances were not quarterly as required; plan to comply in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended submission of legal basis to validate the grant; otherwise expenditure will not be allowed.
observations.managementAction: Sangguniang Bayan Resolution No. 76-2017 dated December 4, 2017 was submitted but AAR for CY 2017 found lack of legal basis; the resolution was forwarded for evaluation.
observations.reason: Still under evaluation; prior AAR noted lack of legal basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended adherence to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1, proper programming and utilization.
observations.managementAction: Portion of 20% fund was obligated and utilized; some reprogramming effected; unexpended balances reported per SCBAA.
observations.reason: Projects still not fully implemented; some funds remain unutilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended BAC to submit required documents and the contracting documents be provided to the Auditor for review and evaluation; require contractors to submit other documents peculiar to contract agreements; Municipal Accountant to refrain from processing claims without complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: Some of the required documents were submitted to the Auditor. The BAC see to it that all required documents are submitted. Comparisons and compliance are being checked. Some required documents were missing but were later submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the MSWDO to submit any Memorandum of Agreement entered into between the Municipality and the recipients relative to the duties and obligations of both parties in relation to the attainment of the objectives of the program.
observations.managementAction: Memorandum of Agreement requirement was submitted to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Engineer to prepare and attach Approved Budget and Contract in required form, Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat and concerned officials to strictly abide with Revised IRR documentary requirements, and concerned municipal officials to immediately submit the lacking documents for further evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that the contractors submitted required documents and BAC is ensuring submission of all documents. Some documents were lacking on CY 2019 contracts but were being prepared/completed.
observations.reason: There were lacking documents noted on CY 2019 contracts as of June 30, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Assessor to pursue titling of the 28 parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to establish absolute ownership and require the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the nature of circumstances as to the mode of acquisition of land not covered by TCT.
observations.managementAction: Some lands already titled (municipal compound, gym and park). The municipal cemetery, temporary settlement area, artesian well and livestock auction market have pending application for Special Patent (July 10, 2019 and January 2, 2020). Remaining land for market site awaiting approved plan from geodetic engineer. The Municipal Accountant indicated tracing old documents for disclosure.
observations.reason: There still remain unaccounted parcels; tracing of old documents ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate request for write-off and forward documents to COA for final authority to write-off; and to start documentation and propose actions on dormant receivables under the General Fund totaling ₱579,859.27.
observations.managementAction: Request for write-off was forwarded for KKK loans; documents already forwarded to COA for action as noted by the Municipal Accountant. Indorsement was made in Nov. 2014 for KKK loans. Documentation still in process for other dormant accounts.
observations.reason: KKK loans write-off submitted; other long outstanding receivables under General Fund still need action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the focal person to spearhead establishment of a GAD database for planning, programming and policy formulation; require concerned GAD Focal Point System personnel to include only eligible expenses in GPB and AR.
observations.managementAction: The Gender Focal Person admitted need for capacity development; some activities undertaken; proposal for organizational structure prepared; re-submission and revision of AR instructed; coordination with DILG ongoing.
observations.reason: Due to lack of personnel and voluminous workload, GAD database and full compliance are still in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict compliance by BAC with COA Circular documentary requirements and submission of lacking documents to Auditor for review and evaluation; management require contractors to submit other documents peculiar to contracts; Municipal Accountant to refrain from processing claims without complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: Required documents were submitted and compliance have been acted upon. Management required contractors to submit documentation; contracts reviewed and additional documents submitted to Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended submission of MOA and supporting documents between Municipality and recipients.
observations.managementAction: MOA and requirement documents were submitted to the Auditor for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: See recommendation above to strictly comply with RA 9184 and COA circulars; submit lacking documents.
observations.managementAction: Management replied that documents were submitted and BAC ensured compliance; some documents were later completed.
observations.reason: There were lacking documents noted on some contracts; completion ongoing.

observations.recommendation: Pursue titling and disclose mode of acquisition in the Notes to FS.
observations.managementAction: Some lands titled; others pending Special Patent applications; municipal accountant to trace old documents.
observations.reason: Some parcels still untitled; documentation in process.

observations.recommendation: Initiate write-off process and submit supporting documents to COA.
observations.managementAction: Request for write-off forwarded; documents submitted to COA for action.
observations.reason: Write-off for KKK loans was forwarded; other dormant receivables still pending action.

observations.recommendation: Create GAD database, include only eligible expenses in GPB and AR; capacitate GFPS.
observations.managementAction: GFPS admitted need for further capacity building; steps taken to revise AR; coordination with DILG ongoing.
observations.reason: GAD activities and database still being developed; full compliance pending.

observations.recommendation: Strict compliance with COA/RA 9184 documentary requirements.
observations.managementAction: Required documents submitted; BAC ensured compliance; audit review performed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Create an Inventory Committee, who shall conduct an actual physical inventory
of all Municipal property and prepare a complete Report on the Physical Count
of Property, Plant and Equipment.;
b. Instruct the GSO-Designate to coordinate with the Accounting Office to establish
the discrepancy between the RPCPPE and the Municipality’s ledger and
reconcile/settle the deficiencies noted so that the balances of PPE accounts as
presented in the financial statements would be accurately and fairly presented.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to determine the
nature of acquisition of the said land owned by the Municipality, and
subsequently record its equivalent peso value to present fairly the Land
account in the financial statements;
• instruct the Municipal Assessor to pursue the titling of the 11 parcels of
land in the name of the Municipality in order to establish absolute
ownership over the said property; and
• require the Municipal Budget to provide appropriation for the cost of
titling the land.

Management’s Comment/s:
The Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant are working hand and
hand to determine the nature of acquisition of the 11 parcels of land owned by the
Municipality. The Municipal Accountant also disclosed that she inherited this
condition upon assumption as Municipal Accountant, same is true with that of the
Acting Municipal Treasurer. The Municipal Accountant also assured that she will
record the said parcels of land as soon as the Municipal Assessor submitted the
necessary documents. Appropriation for the titling of the land will be made for the
budget year 2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, require the Municipal Assessor
and Municipal Budget Officer to coordinate towards the appropriation/budget
preparation and ultimately titling of subject lots. Further, refund the excess
payment amounting to P346,580.00.

Management’s Comment/s:
The Municipal Accountant commented that Sangguniang Bayan Resolution No. 62-
2018 dated June 18, 2018, authorizing the Local Chief Executive to purchase the lot
for the establishment of slaughterhouse, stated that the land to be purchased is
34,658 sq. m. with a value of P9,011,080.00, using this data they arrived at P260.00
per square meter which they used as their basis in the computation of payment for
the said property. In view thereof, she commented that they paid the property at
P260,00 per sq. m. in good faith misinterpreting the report from the Provincial
Appraisal Committee. She added that they are hoping and praying for understanding
their position that there are corrections and errors in the report and is acknowledging
honest mistake made as their part.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team stand firm that the Provincial Appraisal Committee Report should
have been considered by the Municipality of Padre Garcia in the purchase of the
34,658 square meter properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and
other concerned officials to:
• adopt measures and strategies to improve collection efficiency, and in order to
improve the financial position, operation and development of the Municipality.
• to design effective tax strategies to intensify collection of Real Property Taxes,
not only current taxes but also the delinquent taxes in order to generate more
income.
We likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to check from the Provincial Treasurer the status of the rest of the
reported tax delinquencies and the action taken by the latter in the imposition of
the required remedies in the collection of real property taxes against said
delinquent taxpayers.

Management’s Comment/s:
The Municipal Treasurer informed that she prepared and sent demand letters to the
taxpayers of each barangay as a reminder for them to pay their taxes. They had also
conducted a house to house tax campaign to collect these past due accounts. Likewise,
they have posted List of Delinquent Tax Payers at the main entrance of the Municipal
Hall and conspicuous places in each concerned barangay. She had also reported
delinquent taxpayers to the Provincial Government which in turn sent demand letters and
implemented remedies. Warrant of Levy were provided by the Provincial Treasurer. The
provincial government has initiated tax relief program under Provincial Ordinance No. 002
for the year 2018 wherein the collection of interest, penalties and surcharges is
