condoned, cancelled or waived by the provincial government. This program has a great
contribution to encourage delinquent real property tax payers to pay their obligations in
full. She informed that the total delinquency transferred to the Office of the Provincial
Treasurer amounted to P1,703,111.85, with a substantial collection of P1,111,615.92 or
65.26 percent, thereby leaving a balance of P591,495.93. However, their effort to collect
more of these delinquent accounts failed.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant and her staff to continuously coordinate with the Provincial
Accountant and reconcile their records on receivables/payables and their
corresponding payables/ receivables in the books of the Province to establish the
correct balances of the reciprocal accounts by identifying the causes of
discrepancies in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers and to ascertain the legality
and validity of both claims so both parties may make the necessary adjustments
on their respective books of accounts, if warranted.

Likewise, effect the immediate settlement of obligations of each fund for a fair
presentation of the cash position for the three funds.

Management’s Comment/s:
The Municipal Accountant informed that one of her staff has been assigned to reconcile
the municipal records on receivables/payables with that of the records of the province.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Local
Development Council:
a) ensure the 20% Development Fund appropriated out of Internal Revenue
Allotment be optimally utilized and identified programs and activities be
implemented in full to help achieve the desirable outcomes on socio-
economic development and environmental management pursuant to the
DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1;
b) come up with a well-defined mechanism in order that appropriate results
and outputs from the identified priority programs and projects be delivered
efficiently and effectively to the public on time.
c) re-program and utilize the unexpended balances to other developmental
projects.

Management’s Comment/s:
The Assistant Municipal Engineer commented that the delay of implementation of Agri-
enterprise project was due to the unexpected arrival of the fund from the National
government for the construction of the municipal public market which should be
implemented immediately. In addition she assured that all unexpended balances will be
utilized to other developmental projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant
to verify and evaluate the accounts involved in the above transactions and to
prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the adjustments for the stale checks
to present the correct and the reliable balance of the Cash in Bank and Accounts
Payable accounts in the financial statements.

Management’s Comment/s:
The Municipal Accountant commented that she had prepared a Journal Entry Voucher
(JEV) No. 400 dated April 23, 2019 to recognize the amount of stale checks and
remained unclaimed by debiting Cash in Bank and credited Accounts Payable both
amounting P31,067.46 and copy furnished the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) and Technical Working Group (TWG) to:
a. scrutinize the sufficiency of the winning bidder’s manpower and equipment on all
the projects to be awarded and not on an individual project only in cases where the
latter is the lowest bidder in several projects which have similar or overlapping
implementation period. This is to oblige the winning contractor to supplement its
manpower and/or equipment to be utilized in the projects awarded to it or to
disqualify it from obtaining several awards or contracts in conformity with the
provisions of Section 34.1 and 34.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184;
b. monitor contractors with on-going projects with other government agencies and to
coordinate with them involved in implementing infrastructure projects not disclosed
in the Statement of On-going and Completed projects to avoid overlapping and
delay in project accomplishment and/or substandard quality of work;
c. conduct the required processes and procedures in all the stages of the bidding in the
procurement of infrastructure and comply strictly with all the documentary
requirements as mandated by RA 9184. Non-compliance of the required documents
by the prospective bidder shall be a cause for automatic disqualification in the
eligibility and award of contract; and
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to
strictly and closely monitor all projects simultaneously implemented by the contractors
and prepare an individual status report per project to be submitted to the Municipal
Mayor for monitoring purposes and in order to check, verify and evaluate the quality of
work and efficiency of the said contractors.

Management’s Comment/s:
The Municipal Engineer who is also the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman
commented that they follow the implementing rules and regulations in the procurement of
goods, infrastructure and consulting services as mandated by RA No. 9184 from
advertisement/ posting of invitation to bid to the issuance of notice to proceed. In the
evaluation of bid and post qualification, they see to it that all necessary requirements and
completeness of the bid documents is obtained and responsiveness of the bid. With
regards to the evaluation of the adequacy of manpower and equipment submitted by the
contractor, upon verification, they have several equipment utilized besides those
enumerated in the list of equipment submitted. Upon monitoring of their on-going
projects, they have numerous personnel/manpower/workers for the implementation of
each project.
He added that the Office of the Municipal Engineer closely monitor the implementation of
all projects and see to it that all projects are constructed in accordance to design and
specification as well as the timeliness of construction.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate regular bonded employees
who are holders of permanent position to act as collectors/custodians of
government funds. As such, they should be properly bonded pursuant to Section
101 of PD 1445 and Section 305 (f) of RA 7160 and Treasury Circular No. 02-2009
dated August 6, 2009.

Management’s Comment/s:
The Acting Municipal Treasurer commented that she is aware of the situation governing
assignment of Job Order and casual personnel who are not and cannot be bonded due to
the status of their employment in collection duties, however, it cannot be avoided due to
lack of regular bonded employees to serve as collecting officers. Despite of the situation,
she assured that monitoring system is being employed by the Municipal Treasurer’s
Office. Three regular/permanent employees are assigned to monitor and supervise the
JO/casual personnel acting as collectors. So far, they have not yet encountered problems
regarding this situation.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team stand firm as to strict compliance with the requirements of the
provisions of the laws, rules and regulations cited therein.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the General Services
Officer-Designate to initiate immediately the processing of the insurance policies of the
various property of the Municipality, to ensure indemnification or compensation by the Municipality
for any damage to, or loss of, its properties due to fire, earthquake, storm or other
casualty.

Likewise, we recommended that the General Services Officer-Designate prepare the copy of the
latest inventory report with complete list of all insurable properties for indorsement to the
GSIS so that the appraisal thereof and the computation of the amount of insurance
premiums payable by the Municipality could be readily made. Also, the insurance
coverage made with a private company be renewed with the GSIS. Henceforth, strictly
comply with R.A. No. 656, to ensure that the Municipality will be compensated or
indemnified for property damages or losses caused by fire, earthquake and other covered
calamities.

Management’s Comment/s:
The GSO-Designate assured preparation of the latest inventory report with complete list
of all insurable properties for indorsement to the GSIS. He also assured that renewal of
insurance coverage made with a private company will be with GSIS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to compel all barangays to establish
a functional Material Recovery Facility and ensure its continuous and effective
maintenance.

Management’s Comment/s:
The Municipal Mayor shall compel all barangays to establish a functional MRF.
Seminar on Anti-Burning Law will be conducted to remind them of the effect of burning
to ones health and environment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Office of the Human
Resource Management and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to discontinue the
practice of allowing deductions on monthly loan amortizations of government from
the personnel’s payroll other than those mandated by law. Strictly comply with the
GAA provision that monthly take-home pay of employee shall not be lower than
Five Thousand Pesos (₱5,000.00).

Management’s Comment/s:
The HRMO commented that the Municipal Mayor issued OMM Memo No. 18 s. 2019
dated April 17, 2019, requiring strict compliance with the GAA provision that monthly
take-home pay shall not be lower than P5,000.00. The HRMO acknowledged and
promised to conform to the rules and regulation.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the focal person to implement
immediately the required activities on Supplementary Feeding Program based on
the set timeline in order to improve and sustain the nutritional status of the target
children beneficiaries and ultimately contribute to the success of the program as a
whole.

Management’s Comment/s:
The MSWDO/Focal Person commented that the fund for CY 2018 Supplementary
Feeding was downloaded on June 26, 2018. The processing took time due to proper
accounting and bidding procedure. She added that though there was a delay in the
implementation, the feeding program ended within the school year. They will comply with
twice a year liquidation report as required.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal Treasurer
to strictly comply with the requirements of Paragraph Q, Section 2 of COA Circular
No. 92-239, to maintain sound internal control system in the disbursement of
public funds. The perforation or stamping of the word “PAID” should not be limited
only to the disbursement vouchers but should include all its supporting documents
to serve as a control measure in the prevention from reuse of same documents.

Management’s Comment/s:
The Acting Municipal Treasurer informed that she is already complying with the
requirements of stamping the word “PAID” on all disbursement vouchers including all its
supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the honorable members of the
Sanggunian include among its top priorities the construction of a building for the
safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents
generated by the various departments/offices of the Municipality, particularly those
for submission to the audit team, with consideration on the preservation period of
ten (10) years as mandated by law.

Management’s Comment/s:
The Municipal Accountant commented that the LGU of Padre Garcia is currently
undergoing simultaneous construction of office buildings to accommodate different offices
including a store room to safekeep and safeguard the pertinent documents of the LGU.
Plans for renovation of the Old Building will take place after the completion of the
Accounting/Local Civil Registrar Building and provision for COA work station and
storeroom will be a top priority.
Likewise, the Municipal Engineer commented that the Municipality is on its way to the
continuous infrastructure developments and one of its priority is the
construction/rehabilitation/improvement of all its offices in the municipal compound which
is still on-going as of this time. Rest assured that all required offices for both local and
national will be provided by the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: • OIC Municipal Treasurer to determine the actual condition of the property and the property in the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment in order to establish the physical existence and status if serviceable and unserviceable; • Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and General Services Officer to comply with the duties and responsibilities explicitly provided in COA Circular No. 2015-008; • Concerned Municipal officials to ensure that the road networks be properly recorded in the Registry and be transferred to the books of accounts within the target dates or period of four years which ends in CY 2019; and • OIC-Municipal Treasurer to submit the updated Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment for all property and equipment owned by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is monitoring all property and equipment as amended in order to establish the physical existence and condition of all PPE. The concerned officials were aware of their designated duties and responsibilities. The Municipal Accountant has started the transfer of assets. The Municipal Engineer to conducted inventory of local roads and is consolidating all the data and its components as well as the road map. The Municipal Accountant maintains an updated Acknowledgement Receipt for all property and equipment owned by the Municipality.
observations.reason: They are completing all annexes required and once completed, the report in the RPCPPE will be submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to adopt effective and efficient tax strategies and apply various provisions of Republic Act No. 7160 in the collection of real property taxes to avoid accumulation of delinquent accounts. Regularly send notice of delinquency to real property owners especially those with huge balances of unpaid RPT. To enforce collection, avail of the remedies by administrative action through levy on real property or by judicial action.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer prepared and sent demand letters to the taxpayers of each barangay as a reminder for them to pay their taxes. They conducted a house to house tax campaign to collect these past due accounts. They have posted List of Delinquent Tax Payers at the main entrance of the Municipal Hall and conspicuous places in each concerned barangay. She had also reported delinquent taxpayers to the Provincial Government which in turn sent demand letters and implemented remedies. Warrant of Levy were provided by the Provincial Treasurer. The provincial government initiated tax relief program under Provincial Ordinance No. 002 for the year 2018 wherein the collection of interest, penalties and surcharges is condoned, cancelled or waived by the provincial government. The delinquency transferred to the Office of the Provincial Treasurer amounted to P1,703,111.85, with a substantial collection of P1,111,615.92 or 65.26 percent, thereby leaving a balance of P591,495.93. However, their effort to collect more of these delinquent accounts failed.
observations.reason: Their effort to collect more of these delinquent accounts failed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balance of the reciprocal accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds in all funds balances of the reciprocal accounts and to ensure that all transactions pertaining thereto are properly and accurately recorded in the municipality’s books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant prepared JEV Nos. 300-2018-05-01660, 300-2018-05-01661 and 300-2018-05-01758 all dated May 3, 2018 to effect the adjustment in the accounts.
observations.reason: Same finding has been observed in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Municipal Treasurer/ General Services Officer to implement the procurement process to keep track the movement of supplies and materials from receipt until issuance for purposes of accounting and accountability; • initiate the preparation of stock cards for the purchased supplies; • conduct periodic inventory of supplies as prescribed by the above regulation; and • procure, as far as practicable and feasible, directly from the DBM-PS all anticipated common-use supplies, in bulk or three months supply and resort to local suppliers only when not available with said office which was must be duly supported with certification of non-availability.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer/ GSO is monitoring all procurement, the procurement of supplies in bulk is being observed. The GSO-Designate has just started the preparation of stock cards for the purchased supplies. Procurement of supplies in bulk is being observed.
observations.reason: They are still completing stock cards. Procurement of supplies in bulk is being observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: • review and analyze the nature of these payables, exert effort to produce the available documents to support the claims, and if proven to be without valid claims, request for a reversion of resolution from the Sangguniang Bayan for reversion to the unappropriated surplus as provided in Executive Order No. 109 dated July 10, 1993; and submit the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the appropriate adjustment of payables totaling P216,710.00 and terminal leave claims of P3,478,045.47 due to former elected officials and employees who are no longer in the service to verify the correctness of the amount booked up under Accounts Payable and Due to Officers and Employees.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant furnished the Audit Team copy of SB Resolution No. 68-2018 allowing the reversion of P216,710.00 from Accounts Payable to Unappropriated Surplus as provided in Executive Order No. 109 dated July 10, 1993. Terminal leave claims of elected officials and employees showed a balance of P4,841,320.99 with control record as of December 31, 2018. Certificate of Leave Credits due to elected officials and elected employees were submitted by the Human Resource to support the request of P4,841,320.99.
observations.reason: Documents like Certificate of Leave Credits due to elected officials and elected employees were submitted; terminal leave claims are being controlled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the current portion of the loans payable and properly disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the current portion of this account as of December 31, 2018.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared a Journal Entry Voucher to recognize the current portion of the loans amounting to P26,520,713.14 under Non-Current Liability and the current portion was disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements as of December 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to remit the shares of the respective barangays from real property taxes within the prescribed period after the end of each quarter in order that the barangays could use the fund at the earliest possible time and deliver the basic services to their constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant remitted shares of barangays for the 2018 every semester. The Local Chief Executive wants a material or significant amount for its release.
observations.reason: Remittance of barangay shares was made every semester in 2018, due to management wants a material amount for its release.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality submit legal basis that allows granting of Rice Subsidy Allowance to all the municipal officials and employees to validate the propriety of the disbursement of funds, otherwise the expenditure shall not be allowed in audit. We advised the Municipal Mayor to refrain from paying benefits except those expressly authorized by law and require the municipal officials and employees to refund the amount of Rice Subsidy Allowance given to them.
observations.managementAction: Payment of Rice Subsidy Allowance had been stopped. Sangguniang Bayan Resolution No. 76-2017 dated December 4, 2017 authorizing the grant of bonuses in the form of Merchandise Allowance was without support legal basis. Ordinance No. 59-2017 dated December 19, 2017 to supplement the fund which is being used as the source was also reviewed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Budget Officer to stop the practice of charging prior year’s expenses to current year’s appropriations and ensure that all expenses incurred during the year be obligated, paid and taken up in the accounts of that year; b. Municipal Accountant to ensure that all valid obligations not paid shall be taken up as Accounts Payable at year end; c. Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledger for all payments and to double check entries made to avoid errors in recording; and d. Rural Health Physician to refund double payment of honorarium for the month of December 2016 amounting P4,000.00 under Official Receipt No. 22678190 dated July 12, 2018.
observations.managementAction: Charging of prior year’s expenses to current year’s appropriations has been stopped. The Municipal Accountant is maintaining subsidiary ledger for all payments. Payment of Rice Subsidy Allowance stopped and Rural Health Physician refunded the amount of P4,000.00 under Official Receipt No. 22678190 dated July 12, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality meticulously and judiciously consider projects before its inclusion in the Local Development Fund and Annual Investment Program and promptly implement and completed the programs/projects therein in order to achieve the desirable socio-economic and environmental benefits as prescribed of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1. We also required the Municipal Budget Officer to make proper adjustment in the balance of the 20% Development Fund continuing appropriations.
observations.managementAction: The Local Development Council and the Budget Officer had meetings and consultations to justify the need and urgency of the project that is chargeable to the 20% Development Fund. The Municipal Budget Officer made the necessary adjustment in the balance of the 20% Development Fund continuing appropriation. Management justified that the budget for the construction of center island for installation of streetlights which is chargeable to the 20% Development Fund. All unexpended balances will be utilized to other developmental projects.
observations.reason: Proper adjustments of the balance of the 20% Development Fund continuing appropriation has been done; some projects are ongoing or awaiting funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to prepare and submit to the Local Development Council and Local Disaster Risk Management Council the annual LDRRM Plan showing in detail the list of programs, projects and activities and ensure that implementation of the same will be done in compliance with the aforementioned regulation. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for transfers of unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund by year of transfer; the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain Registry of Appropriation, Allotments, and Obligations for RAAO as well as Statement of Appropriations, Commitments and Balances; and disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in order to inform the users of relevant information and fair presentation of financial statements.
observations.managementAction: LDRRMF was included in the preparation of LDRRMIP. The Municipal Accountant maintains Subsidiary Ledgers for transfers of unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund by year of transfer. The Municipal Budget Officer maintains RAAO. The unexpended balance of prior years was included in the preparation of LDRRMIP. The Municipal Accountant disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person and Municipal Budget Officer to prepare GAD budget in accordance with the JMC and to submit to the DILG Regional Office for review and approval. Require the GAD Focal Person to spearhead the establishment of a GAD database to serve as basis for planning and programming and require the concerned GAD personnel to include only eligible expenses in their GPB and AR.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person submitted GAD Plans and Budget to the DILG Regional Office for review and evaluation. The Municipality is establishing a GAD database and will include only eligible expenses in the GPB and AR.
observations.reason: Establishment of GAD database is in the planning stage; some submissions were made to DILG and Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality comply strictly with COA Circular No. 2013-004 on public disclosure and publicity of projects/ programs/projects/ activities to be implemented by the agency for the year and information on the posting of project signboards/public notices within ten days after award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity, as well as the Monthly Report of Publicized projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer has been submitting list of all on-going projects/activities implemented by the agency for the year and information on the posting of project signboards/public notices and monthly reports of publicized projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly comply with the provision of the aforementioned COA Circular and require the contractors to submit the other documents peculiar to the contract agreements. In addition, the Municipal Accountant to refrain from processing claims without complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat submitted the required documents to the Audit Team. Municipal Accountant restrains from processing claims without complete documentation.
observations.reason: Some documents were submitted; some are still being completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer prepare and attach the Approved Budget for the Contract in a form prescribed under DPWH Order No. 22, Series of 2015, copy to Bids and Award Committee (BAC). The BAC and others to strictly abide with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 particularly on documentary requirements. Municipal Accountant to ensure all documentary requirements provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 for a particular transaction were strictly complied with to avoid audit suspension/disallowance. Municipal Accountant to record mobilization fee/15% advance payment to Advances to Contractors and credit this account whenever there is recoupment or progress billing/payment was made.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Engineer and BAC follow procurement rules and have submitted several documents. The Municipal Accountant recorded mobilization advance properly and prepared JEVs to recognize advances and recoupments. Some documents were attached; mobilization was debited to Advances to Contractors.
observations.reason: Sometimes management forgets to attach some documents although they are available; evaluation of MOA undergoing; some adjustments made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the financial statements in accordance with the PPSASs and Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 to assist users in understanding the financial statements and to provide them with information relevant to decision-making.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has complied with the recommendation and observed the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the financial statements in accordance with the PPSASs and COA Circular No. 2016-004.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer conduct regular reconciliation of their records pursuant to Sections 15 and 21 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards I - Presentation of Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Budget Officer agreed for the regular reconciliation of their records. They conducted reconciliation of records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Assessor pursue the titling of the 28 parcels of land in the name of the Municipality in order to establish absolute ownership over the said property. Also, the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the circumstance as to the mode of acquisition of the land not covered with TCT so as not to mislead users of financial statements and appropriate valuation be taken up in the books.
observations.managementAction: The titling of the 28 parcels of land is on-going. The papers are already with the Registry of Deeds. The Municipal Accountant disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements the situation as to the mode of acquisition.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still in process of application for Special Patent. For the purchase of Barangay papers are already with Registry of Deeds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer/ General Services Officer implement the requisition procedures and the reporting on Issuance of Supplies/Materials as required under Sections 114 and 121 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs and the Municipal Accountant draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the appropriate supplies expense accounts based on Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI). Also procure from DBM-PS.
observations.managementAction: Inventory accounting is now effective. The Municipal Accountant implemented the SSMI and JEVs to record supplies as inventory and issuance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to require each end–user units of the Municipality to prepare their respective PPMP to support the budget for their different programs, activities and projects (PPA). We also recommended the BAC Secretariat to review and consolidate the PPMPs into an APP to be approved before any procurement of goods, services and civil works is undertaken.
observations.managementAction: PPMPs were prepared by end-users. The BAC Secretariat reviewed and consolidated the PPMPs into an APP to be approved by the HOPE before any procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the approval by the Sangguniang Bayan for the request for write-off of KKK loans so that the other documents will be forwarded to the Commission on Audit for final authority to write-off; start the documentation of and propose the actions to be taken on the dormant receivables under the General Fund totaling P579,859.27.
observations.managementAction: Request for write-off was made in 2014. Indorsement and attached documents were submitted. There was a resolution requirement authorizing the filing of the request and fee is not yet complied. Attached is a November 6, 2014 re-indorsement requirement to COA.
observations.reason: Attached dated November 6, 2014 re-indorsement to COA requirement for filing fee is not yet complied. Some documents were forwarded to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point Person to submit GAD Plans and Budget to the DILG Regional Office for review and approval; require the GAD Focal Point to spearhead the establishment of a GAD database that will serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and require the concerned GAD personnel to include only eligible expenses in their GPB and AR.
observations.managementAction: Submitted GAD Plan and Budget to DILG Regional Office for review and approval. Establishment of GAD database is in the planning stage and will be included in the overall management information system of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Due to lack of personnel the establishment of GAD database is in the planning stage.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality comply strictly with COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the disclosure and publicity of projects/programs/activities to be implemented by the agency for the year and information on the posting of project signboards/public notices within ten days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity, as well as the Monthly Report of Publicized projects.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Engineer has been submitting the list of on-going government projects/ programs/ activities during the year and information on the posting of project signboards/public notices and monthly reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly comply with COA Circular and other procurement rules and require contractors to submit required documents peculiar to contract agreements; Municipal Accountant to refrain from processing claims without complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: Documents were submitted to the Auditor for review and evaluation. Municipal Accountant refrains from processing claims without complete documentation.
observations.reason: Some documents were submitted; some still outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Accountant to observe the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the financial statements in accordance with PPSASs and COA Circular No. 2016-004.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant complied with the recommendation and observed the disclosure requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to conduct regular reconciliation of their records pursuant to PPSAS I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Budget Officer agreed for the regular reconciliation of their records and reconciliation was carried out.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Assessor to pursue the titling of the 28 parcels of land and the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the circumstance as to the mode of acquisition of the land not covered with TCT and appropriate valuation be taken up in the books.
observations.managementAction: Titling of the parcels of land is on-going. The papers are already with the Registry of Deeds.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still in process of application for the Special Patent; some documents are with Registry of Deeds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer/ GSO to implement requisition procedures and reporting on issuance and the Municipal Accountant to prepare JEVs to record appropriate supplies expense accounts based on SSMI and procure from DBM-PS.
observations.managementAction: Inventory accounting is now effective. The Municipal Accountant maintained SSMI and JEVs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Mayor to direct Municipal Budget Officer to require end-user units to prepare PPMPs and BAC Secretariat to consolidate PPMPs into APP.
observations.managementAction: PPMPs prepared by end-users and consolidated into APP by BAC Secretariat; APP approved before procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Mayor to instruct Municipal Accountant to facilitate approval by Sangguniang Bayan for write-off of KKK loans and start documentation of dormant receivables under General Fund.
observations.managementAction: Request for write-off was made in 2014. Indorsement documents submitted. Some requirements remain to be complied.
observations.reason: Filing fee requirement and some documentation to COA not yet complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended GAD Focal Person to submit GAD Plans and Budget to DILG, establish GAD database, and include only eligible expenses in GPB and AR.
observations.managementAction: GAD Plan and Budget submitted to DILG. Establishment of GAD database is ongoing and will be part of management information system.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel slowed establishment of GAD database.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict compliance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 on publicity of projects and posting of signboards and monthly reports.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Engineer has been submitting list of on-going projects and public notices and monthly reports.
